title: Herbal Gum Infection Creams: 8 Proven Remedies for Fast Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins have antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and fight the infection causing gum problems, making it a good remedy for gum infections.
Quercetin is good for gum infection because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause infection.
Tannins in Aloe Vera have antibacterial properties that help to fight and heal gum infections by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and promoting healthy tissue growth in the affected area.
Polyphenols are good for gum infection because they have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling, kill bacteria, and promote healing in the affected area.
Cineole is good for gum infection because it has antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and kill the bacteria causing the infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is a natural remedy that can help with gum infections. The eucalyptus oil in the cream has strong antibacterial properties, which means it can kill the germs that cause gum infections. This oil can also reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums, making it easier to clean and protect the area. When applied to the affected area, the cream can help to loosen and remove any pus or debris that has built up, promoting healing and reducing the risk of infection. The cooling sensation of the cream can also provide relief from pain and discomfort, making it a soothing treatment for sore and infected gums. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus cream is easy to apply and can be used in combination with other treatments, such as antibiotics or mouthwashes, to help manage gum infections.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with gum infection, start by gently washing and drying the leaves of an Aloe vera plant. Then, using a sharp knife or a spoon, carefully scoop out the clear gel from inside the leaf. Next, mix the Aloe vera gel with coconut oil or olive oil in a bowl to create a smooth paste. Add a few drops of tea tree oil, which has antibacterial properties, to the mixture. Mix well until all the ingredients are combined. Now, using a clean glass jar, transfer the mixture to the jar and store it in the refrigerator. Before applying the cream to your gums, gently massage your gums with your fingers to remove any food particles. Then, take a small amount of the cream and apply it directly to the affected area, gently massaging it in. Leave the cream on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing it off with warm water. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day to help soothe and heal your gum infection.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for gum infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream, also known as tea tree oil cream, is good for gum infections because of its powerful antibacterial properties. The active ingredient, tea tree oil, has been shown to kill the bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus and Staphylococcus. When applied to the affected area, the cream helps to reduce swelling and inflammation, making it easier to manage pain and discomfort. Tea tree oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing redness and irritation. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to keep the gums hydrated and healthy, promoting faster healing and preventing further infection. This makes Melaleuca alternifolia cream a popular home remedy for gum infections.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with gum infection, you will need a few ingredients. First, you will need to extract the gel from an Aloe vera plant, which you can do by cutting off a leaf and scooping out the clear gel inside. Next, you will need to mix this Aloe vera gel with some coconut oil and beeswax in a small saucepan, heating it gently over low heat until the beeswax has melted. After this, you can add some essential oils like tea tree oil and eucalyptus oil, which have antibacterial properties and can help to reduce inflammation and fight gum infection. You can then remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a jar or container. Once it has set, you can apply this Aloe vera herbal cream directly to your gums to help soothe and heal gum infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea cream is good for gum infections because it has strong healing properties. The cream contains powerful compounds called alkylamides and caffeic acid that help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause gum infections. When applied to the gums, these compounds work to calm the infection and prevent further damage. Echinacea purpurea cream also helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain, making it easier to chew and swallow. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help to prevent the infection from spreading, allowing the gums to heal quickly and naturally. The cream is also easy to apply and can be used in combination with other treatments, such as mouthwash or antibiotics, for best results.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream for gum infection, start by extracting the gel from an Aloe vera leaf. Cut the leaf lengthwise and scoop out the clear gel with a spoon. Mix the Aloe vera gel with 1-2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax melts and the mixture is smooth. Remove the saucepan from the heat and add 2-3 drops of tea tree oil, which is known for its antibacterial properties that help fight gum infection. Let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a jar. Once the cream is cool, add a few drops of vitamin E oil to help soothe and protect the gums. Store the cream in the refrigerator and apply it to the affected area 2-3 times a day to help reduce swelling and fight gum infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis cream is good for gum infections because it has healing properties that help to reduce swelling and fight bacteria. The active ingredients in the cream, such as tannins and ursolic acid, have antimicrobial properties that help to kill the germs that cause gum infections. This can help to prevent the spread of the infection and reduce the risk of complications. Salvia officinalis cream also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it easier to eat and talk. Additionally, the cream can help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing irritation and discomfort. The antimicrobial properties of Salvia officinalis cream can also help to prevent the growth of new bacteria, which can help to prevent future gum infections.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Aloe vera herbal cream to help with gum infection, first you need to get some Aloe vera gel from the plant. You can extract it by cutting the leaf and scraping the gel from the inside, then strain it to get rid of any remaining chunks. Next, mix the Aloe vera gel with some coconut oil, beeswax, and essential oils like tea tree oil and peppermint oil in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly until the beeswax melts and the mixture is smooth. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly before transferring the mixture to a bowl. Now, add some baking soda, vitamin E oil, and aloe vera extract powder to the mixture and stir well until it's smooth and creamy. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before applying it to your gums to help soothe and heal gum infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is a popular natural remedy for gum infection. This is because it has powerful anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The cream also contains antimicrobial agents that fight against bacteria and other germs that cause infection. When applied to the gums, it promotes healing, soothing the sore and tender areas. Calendula officinalis cream is also known to stimulate blood flow to the affected area, which helps to bring oxygen and nutrients to the gums, promoting faster recovery. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals, which can slow down the healing process.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with gum infection, you will need Aloe vera gel, coconut oil, beeswax, tea tree oil, and vitamin E oil. First, extract the Aloe vera gel from a mature Aloe vera leaf by cutting off the thick outer skin and scooping out the clear gel inside. Next, mix 100 grams of Aloe vera gel with 50 grams of coconut oil in a small saucepan and heat it gently over low heat until the coconut oil is fully melted. Remove the saucepan from the heat and add 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of vitamin E oil. Stir well until the beeswax is fully dissolved. Let the mixture cool and thicken until it reaches a creamy consistency. Once it's cooled, add a few drops of tea tree oil, which has antibacterial properties that can help fight gum infection. Mix well and transfer the cream into a clean container. This Aloe vera herbal cream can be applied directly to the affected area to help soothe and heal gum infections.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for gum infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is a natural remedy that can help with gum infection. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The cream also contains antioxidants, which help to fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections. Aloe vera is also known to promote healing, so it can help to repair damaged gum tissue. This can be especially helpful when combined with good oral hygiene practices, such as regular brushing and flossing. Additionally, the cream is gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for people who have sensitive gums. It can be applied directly to the affected area to provide relief from symptoms and promote healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make an Aloe vera herbal cream that helps with gum infection, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you will need to extract the gel from an Aloe vera leaf by cutting off the top and bottom of the leaf and carefully removing the outer skin to reveal the clear gel inside. Then, you will need to mix the Aloe vera gel with some coconut oil, beeswax, and essential oils like tea tree oil, which has antibacterial properties that can help fight gum infections. Next, you will need to heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth. Once the mixture has cooled, you can add some vitamin E oil and stir well. Finally, you can pour the cream into a jar or container and store it in the fridge to keep it fresh for up to two weeks. When you need to use the cream, simply apply it to your gums with a clean cotton swab and massage it gently into your gums to help soothe and heal any infection.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for gum infection

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural herb that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including gum infections. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help fight off infection-causing bacteria and reduce swelling. When applied topically as a cream, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with gum infections. Additionally, ginger's antibacterial properties can help combat the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus and Porphyromonas, thereby promoting a healthy and balanced oral environment. Regular application of ginger cream can also help to stimulate blood flow to the gums, promoting healing and tissue repair. Furthermore, ginger's antimicrobial properties can help to prevent the spread of infection to other parts of the mouth, such as the teeth and tongue.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream to help with gum infection, you will need Aloe vera gel, coconut oil, beeswax, tea tree oil, and essential oil of peppermint. First, extract the Aloe vera gel from the plant's leaf by cutting it open and scooping out the clear gel. Next, mix the Aloe vera gel with coconut oil in a bowl, and then add beeswax to the mixture, heating it gently over low heat until the beeswax melts and the mixture becomes smooth. Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool for a while. Once the mixture has cooled slightly, add a few drops of tea tree oil, which has antibacterial properties to help fight the infection, and a few drops of peppermint essential oil for its soothing and cooling effect. Stir well and pour the mixture into a container to let it cool completely before using it on your gums to help reduce swelling and alleviate pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera cream is good for gum infection because it has healing properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums. When you apply aloe vera cream to your gums, the gel inside the cream soothes and calms the area. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling, making it easier to clean and care for the infected area. It also has antibacterial properties that help to fight off the infection and prevent it from spreading. Aloe vera cream can also help to reduce bleeding and ease the discomfort of gum infection. Additionally, aloe vera has antiseptic properties that help to prevent the growth of bacteria and other germs that can cause infection. This is why many people use aloe vera cream to help treat and prevent gum infections.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Aloe vera herbal cream to help with gum infection, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, extract the gel from a fresh Aloe vera leaf by cutting off a small piece and scooping out the clear gel inside. Next, mix 2 tablespoons of the Aloe vera gel with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small bowl. Then, add 1 teaspoon of tea tree oil, which is known for its antibacterial properties that can help fight gum infection. After that, add 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil, which helps to soothe and moisturize the gums. Finally, stir in 2 tablespoons of shea butter or beeswax to thicken the cream. Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, transfer it to a small jar and apply it to your gums using a clean cotton swab 2-3 times a day. This homemade Aloe vera cream can help to reduce inflammation, kill bacteria, and promote healing of gum infections.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for gum infection

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful remedy for gum infections. Two key herbs used in these creams are Eucalyptus globulus and Melaleuca alternifolia. Eucalyptus globulus has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can cause gum infections. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe swollen gums and reduce pain. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help combat gum infections and reduce the risk of further complications. When left untreated, gum infections can lead to more serious health issues, such as tooth loss and even heart problems. Fixing a gum infection is crucial to maintaining good oral health and preventing these complications. By addressing the issue, you can prevent pain, discomfort, and the risk of further health problems. This can improve your life in several ways, including by allowing you to eat and speak comfortably, and by reducing the risk of more serious health issues. Regular use of herbal creams can help prevent gum infections from recurring, and by maintaining good oral health, you can feel more confident in your overall well-being.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal creams to remedy gum infection includes Echinacea purpurea, Eucalyptus globulus, and Calendula officinalis. Echinacea purpurea helps to reduce inflammation and fight off the underlying infection. Its immune-boosting properties help the body to heal the gum tissue faster. Eucalyptus globulus has natural antibacterial properties that help to kill the bacteria causing the infection. It also helps to reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Calendula officinalis is known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe the gums and promote healing. When combined, these herbs create a powerful remedy that tackles the infection from multiple angles, reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and promoting healing.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a gum infection, there are some herbal creams you should avoid using. Peppermint oil (Mentha piperita) is one to steer clear of, as it can make gum problems worse by irritating the gums and making them more sensitive. Similarly, clove oil (Syzygium aromaticum) is not recommended, as it can cause more pain and discomfort in the gums. Another herb to be cautious of is wintergreen oil (Gaultheria procumbens), which contains methyl salicylate, a compound that can irritate the gums and make the infection worse. Ginger oil (Zingiber cassumunar) is not recommended due to its potential to irritate the gums and cause more inflammation. Feverfew (Tanacetum parthenium) is also not recommended, as it can cause mouth sores and make gum infections worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: For gum infections, some natural remedies can help reduce pain and inflammation. One of these remedies is aloe vera juice. Drinking aloe vera juice can help soothe the gums and reduce swelling. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the infection. Some people also use tea tree oil, which has antibacterial properties that can help kill the bacteria causing the infection. Oil pulling with coconut oil is another remedy that can help reduce gum inflammation. This is done by swishing coconut oil in the mouth for a few minutes before spitting it out. Coconut oil has antimicrobial properties that can help kill bacteria and reduce inflammation. Some people also use salt water mouthwash to help reduce gum inflammation and kill bacteria. The salt helps reduce swelling and draws out any pus or bacteria. This can help promote healing and reduce pain.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with gum infection, also known as periodontitis, may experience other health problems due to the spread of bacteria from the infected gums to the rest of the body. One common issue is heart disease, as the bacteria from the infected gums can enter the bloodstream and cause inflammation in the heart. Medicinal herbs like garlic and turmeric have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the heart.  Another issue people with gum infection may experience is diabetes, as the bacteria in the infected gums can increase blood sugar levels. Medicinal herbs like berberine and cinnamon have been shown to have a positive effect on blood sugar levels by increasing insulin sensitivity.  Gum infection can also lead to respiratory problems like bronchitis and pneumonia, as the bacteria from the infected gums can travel through the bloodstream and infect the lungs. Medicinal herbs like thyme and eucalyptus have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat these infections.  People with gum infection may also experience digestive problems like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) due to the inflammation caused by the bacteria in the infected gums. Medicinal herbs like peppermint and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system.  Lastly, gum infection can lead to rheumatoid arthritis, as the inflammation caused by the bacteria can spread to the joints. Medicinal herbs like willow bark and boswellia have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation.  These medicinal herbs can help heal the underlying issues caused by gum infection by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and soothing the digestive system. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
